text.title: Tailwind Token Antipatterns
text.summary: Common failures when integrating design tokens with Tailwind: hardcoded values, opacity modifier breakage, naming drift, and dynamic class assembly issues.

antipattern.description: Hardcoded hex values in tailwind.config.js: colors: { primary: '#1A73E8' }
antipattern.reason: Hardcoded values require a code rebuild for any color change, cannot be overridden at runtime for theming, and are invisible to CSS variable consumers like Storybook or server-side rendering.

antipattern.description: Using opacity modifier syntax (bg-primary/50) with CSS variable token values
antipattern.reason: Tailwind's opacity modifiers require the color value to be in RGB channels format, not a hex string or CSS variable reference. bg-primary/50 silently fails or produces incorrect output when --color-primary is a hex value.

antipattern.description: Token naming mismatch between Figma Variables, Style Dictionary output, and Tailwind config
antipattern.reason: Three different naming conventions (Figma uses /, Style Dictionary uses -, Tailwind uses .) cause drift that makes automated token pipelines fail and requires manual reconciliation on every design update.

antipattern.description: Overwriting the full tailwind.config.js when regenerating tokens from a pipeline
antipattern.reason: Full overwrites destroy manual customizations (custom screens, plugins, content paths). Always merge the generated token block into the existing config using Object.assign or spread, never replace.
JIT cannot statically analyze string templates. The class bg-primary assembled at runtime will not exist in the generated CSS because JIT never saw the complete string bg-primary in a source file.

antipattern.description: Generating a Tailwind v3 config for a Tailwind v4 project (or vice versa)
antipattern.reason: Tailwind v4 uses a CSS-first configuration approach with @theme directive; there is no tailwind.config.js. Generating v3 config for a v4 project produces a file the framework ignores entirely.

rule.statement: To use opacity modifiers with token colors, declare CSS variables in RGB channel format (not hex) and configure Tailwind colors with the RGB variable and opacity fallback.
rule.rationale: Tailwind's opacity modifier injects its own opacity value at the color level. This requires the color to be decomposable into RGB components, which hex values are not at CSS variable evaluation time.

example.note: RGB channels format enabling opacity modifiers
Tailwind config using RGB channels for opacity modifier support
